title: Optical assembly with enhanced surface coupling
abstract: An optical assembly, such as an all-glass optical assembly, with one or more features for enhancing the surface coupling of light into a glass substrate through the use of glass, such as low melting temperature glass, to bond a glass optical feature to a glass substrate, such as with photonic curing.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An optical assembly comprising: a glass substrate comprising a first major surface and an opposing second major surface; and at least one photonic cured glass optical feature proximate at least one of the first major surface and the second major surface of the glass substrate. 2. The optical assembly of claim 1 , wherein the glass substrate is a lightguide comprising a first major surface and a second major surface and the at least one glass optical feature is bonded to at least one of the first major surface and the second major surface of the lightguide. 3. The optical assembly of claims 1 or 2, wherein the at least one glass optical feature comprises a low melting temperature glass or a low liquidus glass. 4. The optical assembly of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the optical assembly comprises two or more optical subassemblies, each optical subassembly comprising at least one glass optical feature bonded to a glass substrate or lightguide. 5. The optical assembly of Claim 4, wherein the two or more optical subassemblies are bonded to each other with photonic curing. 6. The optical assembly of Claim 4, wherein at least one glass interposer is disposed between two optical subassemblies. 7. The optical assembly of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the at least one glass optical feature is selected from the group consisting of diffractive optical features, diffraction gratings, volume gratings, nanogratings, self-organizing gratings, nonlinear gratings, refractive optical features, reflective optical features, scattering 

 optical features, metasurfaces, metamaterials, inverse design, and combinations of the same. 8. The optical assembly of claim 2, wherein the at least one glass optical feature comprises at least one blazed diffraction grating. 9. The optical assembly of claim 8, wherein a reflective layer is disposed on at least one surface of the at least one blazed diffraction grating. 10. The optical assembly of claims 8 or 9, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating is configured to diffract a majority of incident light at a diffraction angle (b) greater than a critical angle (0 C ) for total internal reflection within the lightguide. 1 1. The optical assembly of any one of claims 8-10, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises diffracted orders (m): m > , wherein d is a groove periodicity of the blazed diffraction grating and l is a wavelength of light diffracted by the blazed diffraction grating. 12. The optical assembly of any one of claims 8-1 1 , wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises greater than or equal to 5 diffracted orders (m). 13. The optical assembly of any one of claims 8-12, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises a blaze angle (QB) ranging from about 10Â° to about 40Â°. 14. The optical assembly of claim 10, wherein the diffraction angle (b) is greater than about 40Â°. 15. The optical assembly of any one of claims 8-14, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises a groove periodicity (d) ranging from about 2 pm to about 400 pm. 16. The optical assembly of any one of claims 8-15, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises a thickness (t) ranging from about 0.1 pm to about 100 pm. 17. The optical assembly of claim 9, wherein the reflective layer comprises a metallic film. 18. The optical assembly of claims 9 or 17, wherein the reflective layer comprises a thickness ranging from about 0.1 pm to about 100 pm. 19. The optical assembly of Claim 3, wherein the low melting temperature glass or low liquidus glass is selected from the group consisting of tin fluorophosphate glass, tungsten-doped tin fluorophosphate glass, chalcogenide glass, tellurite glass, borate glass, and phosphate glass. 20. The optical assembly of any one of claims 1 -19, wherein the optical assembly is optically coupled to at least one light source, and wherein the at least one light source is optically coupled to the at least one glass optical feature. 21. The optical assembly of Claim 20, wherein the light source is associated with a head-mounted display system, a heads-up display system, a virtual reality system or an augmented reality system. 22. The optical assembly of Claim 8, wherein the optical assembly is optically coupled to at least one light source, and wherein the at least one light source is optically coupled to the first major surface of the lightguide in a region proximate the at least one blazed diffraction grating. 23. The optical assembly of Claim 22, wherein the at least one light source is positioned in overlying registration with the at least one blazed diffraction grating. 24. The optical assembly of claims 22 or 23, wherein the at least one light source emits light having a wavelength (l) ranging from about 350 nm to about 500 nm. 25. The optical assembly of any one of claims 22 to 24, wherein light from the at least one light source is incident on the at least one blazed diffraction grating at an entry angle (a) equal to about 0Â°. 26. The optical assembly of Claim 2, wherein at least one of the first major surface and the second major surface of the lightguide comprises at least one recessed region. 27. The optical assembly of Claim 26, wherein the at least one glass optical feature is disposed within the at least one recessed region and bonded to the lightguide. 28. The optical assembly of Claim 27, wherein at least a portion of the at least one recessed region not occupied by the glass optical feature is filled with a fill glass comprising at low melting temperature glass or a low liquidus glass. 29. The optical assembly of Claim 28, wherein an outer surface of the fill glass is coplanar with the first major surface or the second major surface of the lightguide. 30. The optical assembly of Claim 29, wherein a glass optical feature is bonded to the outer surface of the fill glass. 31. An optical assembly comprising: a glass substrate comprising a first major surface and an opposing second major surface; and at least one glass optical feature; and a glass member disposed between the glass optical feature and the glass substrate, and wherein the glass member is bonded to at least one of the glass optical feature and the glass substrate with photonic curing. 32. The optical assembly of claim 31 , wherein at least one of the glass optical feature and the glass member comprises a photonic cured glass optical feature comprising a low melting temperature or low liquidus glass. 33. The optical assembly of claims 31 or 32, wherein the glass member is a layer or thin film. 34. The optical assembly of claim 33, wherein the layer or thin film is a sputter deposited layer or thin film. 35. The optical assembly of any one of claims 31 to 34, wherein the optical assembly consists essentially of glass. 36. The optical assembly of any one of claims 31 to 35, wherein the glass substrate is a lightguide comprising a first major surface and an opposing second major surface. 37. The optical assembly of any one of claims 31 to 36, the optical assembly further comprises two or more optical subassemblies, wherein each optical subassembly comprises at least one glass optical feature bonded to a glass substrate. 38. The optical assembly of Claim 37, wherein the two or more optical subassemblies are bonded to each other with photonic curing. 39. The optical assembly of Claim 37, wherein at least one glass interposer is disposed between two optical subassemblies. 40. The optical assembly of any one of claims 31 to 39, wherein the at least one glass optical feature is selected from the group consisting of diffractive optical features, diffraction gratings, volume gratings, nanogratings, self-organizing gratings, nonlinear gratings, refractive optical features, reflective optical features, scattering optical features, metasurfaces, metamaterials, inverse design, and combinations of the same. 41. The optical assembly of claim 36, wherein the at least one glass optical feature comprises at least one blazed diffraction grating. 42. The optical assembly of claim 41 , wherein a reflective layer is disposed on at least one surface of the at least one blazed diffraction grating. 43. The optical assembly of claims 41 or 42, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating is configured to diffract a majority of incident light at a diffraction angle (b) greater than a critical angle (0 C ) for total internal reflection within the lightguide. 44. The optical assembly of any one of claims 41 to 43, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises diffracted orders (m): m wherein d is a groove periodicity of the blazed diffraction grating and l is a wavelength of light diffracted by the blazed diffraction grating. 45. The optical assembly of any one of claims 41 to 44, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises greater than or equal to 5 diffracted orders (m). 46. The optical assembly of any one of claims 41 to 45, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises a blaze angle (QB) ranging from about 10Â° to about 40Â°. 47. The optical assembly of claim 43, wherein the diffraction angle (b) is greater than about 40Â°. 48. The optical assembly of any one of claims 41 to 47, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises a groove periodicity (d) ranging from about 2 pm to about 400 pm. 49. The optical assembly of any one of claims 41 to 48, wherein the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises a thickness (t) ranging from about 0.1 pm to about 100 pm. 50. The optical assembly of claim 42, wherein the reflective layer comprises a metallic film. 51. The optical assembly of claims 42 or 50, wherein the reflective layer comprises a thickness ranging from about 0.1 pm to about 100 pm. 52. The optical assembly of claim 32, wherein the low melting temperature or low liquidus is glass selected from the group consisting of tin fluorophosphate glass, tungsten-doped tin fluorophosphate glass, chalcogenide glass, tellurite glass, borate glass, and phosphate glass. 53. The optical assembly of any one of claims 31 to 52, wherein the optical assembly is optically coupled to at least one light source and wherein the at least one light source is optically coupled to the at least one optical feature. 54. The optical assembly of claim 53, wherein the light source is associated with a head-mounted display system, a heads-up display system, a virtual reality system or an augmented reality system. 55. The optical assembly of claim 41 , wherein the optical assembly is optically coupled to at least one light source, and wherein the at least one light source is optically coupled to the first major surface of the lightguide in a region proximate the at least one blazed diffraction grating. 56. The optical assembly of claim 55, wherein the at least one light source is positioned in overlying registration with the at least one blazed diffraction grating. 57. The optical assembly of claims 55 or 56, wherein the at least one light source emits light having a wavelength (l) ranging from about 350 nm to about 500 nm. 58. The optical assembly of any one of claims 55 to 57, wherein light from the at least one light source is incident on the at least one blazed diffraction grating at an entry angle (a) equal to about 0Â°. 59. The optical assembly of Claim 36, wherein at least one of the first major surface and the second major surface of the lightguide comprises at least one recessed region. 60. The optical assembly of Claim 59, wherein the at least one glass optical feature is disposed within the at least one recessed region and bonded to the lightguide. 61. The optical assembly of Claim 60, wherein at least a portion of the at least one recessed region not occupied by the glass optical feature is filled with a fill glass comprising a low melting temperature glass or low liquidus glass. 62. The optical assembly of Claim 61 , wherein an outer surface of the fill glass is coplanar with the first major surface or the second major surface of the lightguide. 63. The optical assembly of Claim 62, wherein a glass optical feature is bonded to the outer surface of the fill glass. 64. A method of making an optical assembly, the method comprising: bonding at least one of a glass optical feature and a glass member to at least one of a first major surface or a second major surface of a glass substrate with photonic curing. 65. The method of claim 64, wherein the method comprises bonding a glass optical feature to a first major surface or a second major surface of a glass substrate with photonic curing. 66. The method of claim 64, wherein the method comprises bonding a glass member to a first major surface or a second major surface of a glass substrate and bonding a glass optical feature to the glass member, wherein the glass member is bonded to at least one of the glass optical feature and the glass substrate with photonic curing. 67. The method of any one of claims 64 to 66, wherein the glass substrate comprises a lightguide comprising a first major surface and a second major surface. 68. The method of any one of claims 64 to 67, wherein the glass optical feature comprises a photonic cured glass optical feature comprising a low melting temperature or low liquidus glass. 69. The method of claim 65, wherein the method comprises bonding two or more optical subassemblies to each other with photonic curing, wherein each optical subassembly comprises at least one glass optical feature bonded to a glass substrate. 70. The method of claim 67, wherein at least one of the first major surface and the second major surface of the lightguide comprises at least one recessed region and the method comprises bonding at least one glass optical feature to the at least one recessed region with photonic curing. 71. The method of claim 70, wherein the method further comprises filling at least a portion of the at least one recessed region not occupied by the glass optical feature with a fill glass comprising a low melting temperature glass or low liquidus glass. 72. The method of claim 70, wherein the method comprises configuring an outer surface of the fill glass to be coplanar with the first major surface or the second major surface of the lightguide and bonding a glass optical feature to the outer surface of the fill glass. 73. The method of claim 67, wherein the at least one glass optical feature comprises at least one blazed diffraction grating. 74. The method of claim 73 wherein the method further comprises: applying a reflective film to at least one surface of the at least one blazed diffraction grating; optically coupling at least one light source to a second major surface of the glass substrate; and configuring the at least one blazed diffraction grating to diffract a majority of incident light from the at least one light source at a diffraction angle (b) greater than a critical angle (0 C ) for total internal reflection within the glass substrate. 75. The method of claim 74, wherein configuring the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises at least one of mathematical estimation or modeling to determine the blaze angle (QB) of the blazed diffraction grating for a given wavelength (l) of light from the at least one light source. 76. The method of claims 74 or 75, wherein configuring the at least one blazed diffraction grating further comprises selecting at least one of a groove periodicity (d), thickness (t), and facet angle (0F) of the blazed diffraction grating. 77. The method of claim 76, wherein configuring the at least one blazed diffraction grating comprises depositing a glass layer on a grating mold to produce the blazed diffraction grating comprising the selected groove periodicity (d), blaze angle (0B), thickness (t), and facet angle (0F). 78. The method of any one of claims 74 to 77, wherein disposing the at least one blazed diffraction grating on the first major surface of the glass substrate comprises: depositing a glass layer on a grating mold, contacting the glass layer with the first major surface of the glass substrate, 

 bonding the glass layer with the first major surface, and removing the grating mold to produce the at least one blazed diffraction grating. 79. The method of claim 78, wherein depositing the glass layer on the grating mold comprises vapor deposition or sputtering.
